entry.title: Re: Standing out as a sustainable supplier with SAP Business Network and EcoVadis
entry.content: Hi @amandalaldin , is there more documentation available on the addon for SAP Ariba? Is there also information on how this information can be further integrated with SAP ERP and if this is SAP Standard?

entry.title: Re: Eclipse: How to see / change function module document...
entry.content:
F2 - if you want to display it. Click on "Longtext Documentation".

SE37 to maintain the FM.

entry.title: Re: Create new report/period in SAP Disclosure Management...
entry.content:
Hi Guido,

Thanks for your answer.

But once I copy to a new period, I must always keep it that way, because as I must have the same report name (consolidated/companies changing only the period), I could not create a company report (period ex. 2024_EMP100) in a period 2024, because this 2024 would be the consolidated period (word and datacache will have the same name in all). I understand that this is the only way I can use roll forward. Otherwise, I would have to create all reports (word/datacache) with different names to be able to use them in the same periods (2024, 2024Q1, 2024Q2, ...).

I would really appreciate anyone who could give me more options on how these scenarios are configured on clients.

Regards,

Felipe B.

entry.title: Re: The Power of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Processes - Manage In-House Repair
entry.content:
Hi,

I am new in SAP S4/HANA  Service Management.

Please provide the in-house repair process and onsite repair service configuration document along with all the required master data

entry.title: Sales Intercompany with WBS Element - VF156 error
entry.content:
Greetings,

I have an intercompany service sales scenario, that is, a plant in the group sells a service to the customer, but it will be another plant in the group that will provide the service.

I believe that the problem is in the accounting of the WBS element that is assigned to the plant. As the sales order VA01 is assigned to a sales organization/company, but the item was assigned to the plant that will provide the service, when trying to invoice the sales order I get the error:

Spoiler
VF156 No WBS billing element found while substituting WBS element 00000200.

The SAP Note 672929 already described in other topics left me with more questions than answers. What should be done?
Should the SAP PS consultant configure it so that when it is an Intercompany process, the cost accounting does not happen in the WBS element, but in a higher structure?

Thanks in advance 🙂

entry.title: Authorizations for Managing KPIs and Reports (F2814)
entry.content:
Hello,

im trying to restrict which kpi groups/kpi's can be created/updated/deleted using the fiori App "Managing KPIs and Reports (F2814)" by specific user groups using authorization objects.
After several tests and using the authorization trace i came to the conclussion, that no authorization object is checked when creating/updating/deleting KPI Groups, KPIs or Reports.

Is this behavior intended or is there any way to restrict who can update/delete already exisiting KPI Groups/KPIs/Reports or at least restrict the KPI Groups/KPIs/Reports visible in the list?

best regards

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to embed a program or transaction into another program?
entry.content:
submit <report> with <parameter> = <value> and return.

Something like that?

entry.title: Re: SBO Mail not working in New installation 10.0 FP2402
entry.content: Yes, I have followed the guided answers and some specific notes that I already knew, but it is not resolved. In the FP2208 it´s working eficiently.

entry.title: MDK ERROR Could not create the store. Reason -96 (ENGINE_ALREADY_RUNNING)
entry.content: Hi expert i having the next problem

entry.title: Re: Linking Project WBS to Timesheets in the "Manage My T...
entry.content: If you staff a resource in a Customer Project the assignment will automatically show up in the timesheet app.

entry.title: Re: Derive Funds Center against WBS Element in CJ20N
entry.content: Please add the ABAP tag to your post,

entry.title: Re: Field symbol with component MARA-MATKL
entry.content:
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_mara> TYPE mara.
DATA: l_mtart TYPE mara-mtart.

l_mtart = '5'.

<fs_mara>-mtart = l_mtart.

" ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

entry.title: Re: Field symbol with component MARA-MATKL
entry.content: <fs_mara>-mtart = l_mtart. will not work because the component is "MARA-MTART", If the component is "MTART" only your solution will work

entry.title: Expert Deep Dive Live Session: Learn more about SAP S/4HANA Service & Asset Manager - Register now!
entry.content:
With the release of SAP Service and Asset Manager 2305, we continue the persona-centric support of SAP Service and Asset Manager by offering a new persona to support the Enterprise Health and Safety industry. The mobile solution for SAP Service and Asset Manager will now support SAP Work Clearance Management by offering mobile Lockout/Tagout capabilities for customers using existing SAP WCM on SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA.

The live session “Inventory Clerk and Safety Technician personas with SAP S/4HANA Service & Asset Manager” will provide a clear insight into how the mobile solution can be used by different kinds of users, for example the Inventory Clerk and Safety Technician personas.

After participating, you will be able to understand how the mobile solution can be effectively used by different user personas within the context of SAP Service and Asset Manager, enabling them to optimize inventory management and safety processes in real-world scenarios.

Next dates:

  • June 20, 2024 Jun 20, 2024, 17:00 - 18:00 GMT+2
  • Sep 19, 2024, 17:00 - 18:00 GMT+2

Don't miss this great opportunity - Register now!

entry.title: Shape the Future of SAP Solutions: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative 2024 Cycle 2
entry.content:
Exciting news for our valued SAP customers and partners! The second cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative in 2024 has officially kicked off today, bringing with it 51 new projects to fuel innovation and collaboration.

We understand the importance of staying ahead of the curve in today's fast-paced business landscape. That's why we're excited to offer you the opportunity to gain early insights into SAP's product developments. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you'll have the chance to work directly with our developers and product managers, shaping the future direction of our products.

But time is limited! The projects will be open for customer registration until July 12, 2024. Don't miss out on this opportunity to make your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP's products and solutions.

To help you navigate through the projects, we've compiled a comprehensive list for your convenience. Check out the whole list here or follow the following links to explore the possibilities:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • AI assistant for General Ledger Accountant
  • AI-based warehouse order streaming, task optimization and resource planning
  • Availability Check in Two-Tier ERP Subsidiary Business
  • Basic End-to-end make-to-order / assemble-to-order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • GRC 2026 for SAP GRC Solutions Private Cloud Edition / On-Premise
  • Intelligent assistant capabilities for Project Systems in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Lockout Tagout / Isolation Management in Permit to Work
  • Risk and Assurance Reporting supported by AI
  • SAP Project and Resource Management - Scheduling of projects
  • Taulia receivables financing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Adaptive User Interface powered by GenAI
  • AI Agents - A Low-Code / No-Code Process Automation Innovation
  • Enterprise automation with SAP: Integrate - Identify - Automate
  • Governance Blueprints & Exchange - Govern and monitor developments with SAP Build
  • Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience on SAP BTP
  • SAP Business Technology Platform - Data Protection and Privacy services
  • SAP Gateway Fiori Notifications
  • SAP S/4HANA Extensibility using SAP Build, SAP Build Code and ABAP
  • SAPUI5 2.x

AI

  • Automotive Industry: Explore AI Use Cases in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Insights into SAP BTP AI Innovations

Lifecycle Management

  • SAP Focused Run
  • Simplified Implementation Experience in SAP Cloud ALM

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)
  • User Validation for new SAP Datasphere homepage

User Assistance

  • Moving features into the Manager component of SAP Enable Now
  • Support of generative AI in SAP Enable Now

SAP Globalization Services

  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Public Edition
  • Note Analyzer Improvements

SAP Concur

  • Concur Expense - "Expense Reimagined" Concept
  • Concur Travel and Concur Request - Pre-booking approval
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Company Notes on the SAP Concur Homepage
  • SAP Concur Solutions – Concur Budget
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Concur Home Page Redesign
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Extensibility and External Integration
  • SAP Concur Solutions – Ideas for Future of Travel and Expense Card Spend Controls
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Joule (Digital Assistant)
  • SAP Concur Solutions – Support Knowledge Base Articles- Customer Experience and Feedback
  • SAP Concur Solutions – The Future of Data Delivery Service
  • SAP Integration with SAP Concur Solutions - Accruals Handling
  • SAP Integration with SAP Concur Solutions - Payroll integration

SAP Ariba

  • Embedded Virtual Card Payments for SAP Ariba Procurement and SAP Business Network using Taulia
  • SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management - Potential UI enhancements
  • Shape with us the future of mobile procurement

SAP Learning

  • Protean

Customer Experience

  • Field Service Management - Horizon adoption

SAP Integration Experience Feedback goes SAP Customer Engagement Initiative

  • Assisted Assessment of End-to-End implementation based on SAP Ariba Guided Buying
  • Hands-on System Experience of the Integrated Business Process Order to Cash within Lead to Cash 
  • Hands-on-System Experience of the Intelligent Enterprise Scenario - External Workforce

Others

  • Industrial Metaverse
  • SAP Cloud ALM or Implementation: Customer Engagement Council

We believe that collaboration is key to unlocking innovation. By joining forces with our customers and partners, we can collectively shape the future of SAP’s products and change the way businesses operate.

So, mark your calendars, gather your team, and let's embark on this journey together. Together, we can drive progress, transform industries, and create a brighter future.

Don't delay – register today on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2024!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: sap-outbound-id in a different url parameter
entry.content:
Hi,

In content studio, when you toggle "Outbound ID for Consuming App", this appends the url parameter sap-outbound-id .

You can also use your own "name of link parameter" with "value of link parameter" to add url parameters with the name of your choice.

I would require a link parameter named "cf1" with the sap-outbound-id as its value.

How can I achieve this? I do not seem to find the outbound id in the list of fields for the "value of link parameter"...

Context is that I want to do a survey with Pointerpro. Pointerpro can recognize url parameters, but they need to be in parameters called cf1, cf2, cf3, ... . The url parameter "sap-outbound-id" is not recognized by Pointerpro. 

I looked in the custom logic "Adjust Hyperlink Parameters While Sending Emails" but there it seems mostly about the google analytics parameters, and I also don't see at first sigh a variable that contains the outbound id, but I'm not sure about that.

 

Thanks for your advice,

Kr

Joyca

entry.title: Re: How to enable users to work with the message dashboard
entry.content: Hi @brittamangei - can you please confirm from what S/4HANA on-prem release assignment of roles to recipients for AIF Monitoring is available? My customer is using S/4HANA 2020 - the option to assign roles to recipients is there, and we have entries stored in the underlying table, however it doesn't seem to work.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
hi pokrakam supporting you and the community with current versions of sapgui is ongoing, it's not forgotten.

It takes some more days due to mandatory process steps.

Regards Ralf

entry.title: ADD NEW ROW IN (REQUEST FOR QUOTATION)RFQ SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I would like to add a row 'Discount' in Request for Quotation (RFQ) so that when a vendor opens the link they will be able to view and fill the field.

entry.title: How to configured XBRL options in SAP DM?
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I'm at a client with standard financial reporting scenarios and an XBRL. I need support to create this XBRL scenario.

Please, does anyone have any "how to" for configuring SAP DM XBRL functionality (word, excel, datacache, taxonymy, XBRL mapper, etc.)?

I would really appreciate it.

Felipe B.

SAP Disclosure Management

entry.title: Re: Introducing RAP Business Events
entry.content:
So many "former members" here... 🙄 Keep your profiles alive, people...

And btw, the link to the RAP Guide is broken too.

entry.title: Re: Internal Reconciliation of Journal Entries
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Depleting Inventory - notification to charge customer
entry.content:
Hello - 

There are a few products that we order that are "customer specific", which means that the raw material only goes into 1 or 2 finished goods, for 1 customer.  

For those instances, we have a supply agreement in place with the customer, saying that if the product expires, or they discontinue using that item, that they would be responsible for the balance of inventory.  

Is there a way in SAP, (not in an attachment in MM03) - that when the item is up for expiration, or when the item will be discontinued, a pop up comes up, referencing something like: "customer specific material, charge customer".

entry.title: Re: Not possible to display SAC Live BW Query Texts in Ge...
entry.content: Yep, this wortks, "User Preference" is not a text only but a feature. In SAC You than toggle the profile parameter Language Setting for differnt languages.

entry.title: BAI Code 447 contain Company ID = unique Numeric and Alphanumeric Number for every customer,
entry.content:
Hi BAI Expert,

We have BAI 447 contain Company ID = 5555566257, Ex. COMPANY ID=7596001874, few Company ID is : Alphanumeric like COMPANY ID=APID879070, where every company id is unique in this case what should i do to configure to post every Company ID while upload FF_5, Bank BAI file.

 

Thanks

Pranav K

entry.title: SAP Personas hide fields and f4 help.
entry.content:
Hello,

is it possible to hide one field with a PFCG Role/Group for certain users. they should't see the field and f4 help. 

It is a SAP Standard APP.

I will also hide the F4 Help for a Field e.g. Material with PFCG Role.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Header "Cookie" converted to SHA256 automatically in CPI
entry.content:
Hi, a cookie is set by the target server upon authentication... How come you want to statically set this? It usually refers to an http session (on the server) which expires after a while. So it should be retrieved dynamically. Does your API vendor support e.g. Oauth 2.0 authentication or similar? Then you should start from there.

Usually, you don't have to worry about cookies. The http adapter will set it automatically when you reuse the session. See runtime configuration:

entry.title: Re: Field symbol with component MARA-MATKL
entry.content:
data lt_mara type table of mara.

lt_mara = value #( ( mtart = '5' ) ).

assign lt_mara[ mtart = '5' ] to field-symbol(<fs_mara>).
cl_demo_output=>display( <fs_mara>-mtart ).

 

Improved 🙂 Will that help?

entry.title: Re: Pricing Procedure determination in Purchase info record
entry.content:
PIR pricing procedure and base price condition and Supplement Condition Determination-

  • PIR pricing procedure is triggered/controlled by regular pricing procedure.

 

 

 

 

  • For example , we need to have ZIP0 conditions types in your PIR condition supplement, it should have the vendor schema and Porg schema accordingly which can trigger the procedure which has Condition types like ZIP0.
  • Search the condition type e.g. ZIP0  for respective Procedure in Table T683S.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Now change vendor schema to Z1 which has a procedure which has the required  condition type ; now PIR condition supplement changes PB00 to ZIP0.

 

 

 

 

 

 

                                                 End

entry.title: Re: Eddit outbound delivery extension field after released
entry.content: Dear all, I am also looking for a solution to edit a custom field on the outbound delivery after releasing it. One question regarding your below solution: wouldnt the undorelease/release always create additional Finance documents? I dont think this would be acceptable for my client. Best regards Jan

entry.title: Revenue Recognition - Cost-Based Percentage of Completion Methods with different WBS
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

I created a revenue project and assigned a recognition key (EPMFC) in contract type. 

 

Eg. Project name - R.RPJ.1

Revenue Plan WBS - R.RPJ.11 .001 >> 1000 SGD

Cost Plan WBS - R.RPJ.12 .001 >> 700 SGD

We have uploaded the financial plan data into SAP. And also posted cost journal entries, opened the sales order and billing posted to accounting as well.

When we run the revenue recognition (with these two different WBS), we got the error and couldn't run successfully. The question is can we run the cost-based revenue recognition with different WBS? 

Note: When I ran with same WBS code, it was successfully completed and generate RR documents correctly.

 

It would be appreciated if you answer my question.

 

Best Regards,

Phoo

entry.title: Re: Opensesame New Updated Integration
entry.content:
Thank you for posting this question. I just recently imported 140+ OpenSesame courses and found we could not add Spanish translations to the multilanguage courses. This is an issue because it negatively impacts the ability to identify these course using Spanish. We were told the direct integration would address this but the options to migrate to the direct integration are not good. We have over 300 OpenSesame courses so the LOE to do either of these options is daunting.

The following is from OpenSesame......SuccessFactors Transition Options 1. Start fresh (Recommended) Deactivate all of your existing OpenSesame courses and send these courses through the new integration. This will ensure all course metadata updates and course retirements are reflected in your learning platform. Additionally, this will ensure courses are not duplicated when sent through the new integration. To ensure learners do not lose their progress, we advise setting a date by which all learners should complete their in-progress courses, then deactivating the legacy OCN courses. Reporting for the courses previously synced through the legacy integration will be available in your OpenSesame Activity Report. FAQ Q: How do I remove the courses from SuccessFactors? A: You can remove all content from an integration using the Activate/Deactivate OCN Courses functionality Q: What about my learning paths / curriculum / assignments? A: You will need to rebuild these using the newly synced content Time Commitment ● 1-2 calls with OpenSesame to set up the integration (sandbox/test environment optional) ● 1 hour to deactivate or retire old OpenSesame content (this can be done in bulk) ● X hours to organize new content into libraries / categories / etc. 2. Use the integration for new content If you’re not ready to refresh your full catalog, you can implement the new integration and use it to only sync new courses. Please note that sending courses that have been sent via the old integration will cause two versions of the course to be available in your learning platform. Learners in-progress on previously sent courses will be able to continue in their courses. Reporting for the uploaded courses and courses synced through the integration will be available in your OpenSesame Activity Report. Course retirement communication will continue for courses synced and will need to be retired within SuccessFactors. FAQ Q: How can I make sure I’m not syncing the same course twice? A: You will need to manually validate that you don’t select a course that has been previously sent Q: What will happen if I sync the same course twice? A: The course will be in SuccessFactors twice Time Commitment ● 1-2 calls with OpenSesame to set up the integration (sandbox/test environment optional) ● Ongoing time to make sure no duplicate courses are sent via the new integration

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - script for charts does not work anymore
entry.content:
Hello,

I have added a function that allows you to change the number unit from millions to thousands and back again by clicking on a symbol/text (for the whole story).

So far the following script worked:

Chart_1.getNumberFormat().setScaleUnit(NumberFormatScaleUnit.Million, Feed.ValueAxis);

For tables it still works without problems, but for charts it no longer works.

Does anyone have an idea why this could be?

Many thanks in advance

Best regards
Philipp

entry.title: Re: Field symbol with component MARA-MATKL
entry.content: thanks for trying but still not working

entry.content:
Hello,

Which port is being used? 

Jesper

entry.title: Re: Introduction to MySelf
entry.content: Hello @m_sarosh welcome to SAP Community and thanks for your introduction. I am Svea Becker from the SAP Community team. I would like to make you aware of our location groups, which are designed to connect local people. Find out if there is one group close by where you live and join it!

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@Jan_D1 
And don't forget to join the Wien Group, which is designed to connect local people! Simply join it and find out where the next physical meetup, like SAP Stammtisch, is 😊

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi Suzanne,

I have some queries regarding the assessment:

1. Will it be a proctored exam?

2. What will be the duration?

3. How many questions will there be?

4. Will there only be MCQs like in the SAP certification (proctored) exam?

5. Could you please help with some sample questions?

Thank you very much in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to add company logo on org chart print-outs?
entry.content:
It is possible to add company logo on Org Chart print outs. 
Go to Company Info from the My profile
Select Company Info
From the Org Chart, scrol al the way to the right and find a an arrow. Click on the arrow and the Export org chat wondow will open. Select Format as PDF.

entry.title: Re: Profitability analysis not active
entry.content:
Kindly use the below path to assign operating concern with controlling area

Enterprise Structure > Assignment > Controlling > Assign Controlling Area to Operating Concern

entry.title: Re: Exposing a SOAP service in BTP, ABAP
entry.content:
Thank you - I was able to publish the service locally!

Is there documentation what the adminstrator has to do afterwards? The node "Supported Protocols and Authentication Methods" does not mention SOAP as an inbound connection

entry.title: test
entry.content: test

entry.title: Re: How to add search function in MC86 transaction.
entry.content: As nothing is coded in standard for this function, you have to find some Enhancement Points to add this function to the program (and substitute status to add the new function code)

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hello @Krutika_More,

happy to answer your questions:

So when you want to stay certified and extend your certification validity with the assessment it will be:

1. not proctored. It is a quizz that you can do with unlimited trails anytime. 

2. it will take you about 30 minutes to answer the questions.

3. there will be around 8-12 questions to answer. 

4. and yes, there will be multiple-choice questions to answer. 

5.  the sample questions are offered to prepare for the bigger certification exam.

You find it on the certification pages next to the cut score information on the top of the screen when clicking on "View sample questions" . For example here: 

 

Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: SAP Datasphere connection to AWS Aurora postgresql
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We need a connection from SAP Datasphere to AWS Aurora Postgresql. Is it possible through a generic JDBC connection?

1.Open <DPAgent_root>/camel/adapters.xml and uncomment the configuration of Camel JDBC Adapter.

2.Download the appropriate JDBC file, and copy it to the <DPAgent_root>/camel/lib directory.

3. Restart DP Agent

4. Create remote source from SAP Hana database explorer

Thanks in advance,

Antonio

entry.title: storie report position
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, does anyone know how I can get the following data for a storie report:

- If the position is in Vacant status, bring the time it is vacant, today from the org chart we see it as the position has vacant status since and calculate the time, example: 8 months and 5 days. how can I capture this data for storie report?

- It is possible to create a column within the report, where every time a user downloads the report it will bring the date of execution, i.e. if today June 10 I run the report, when viewing this I will find a column with the date on which it was executed.

I would appreciate your support

SAP SuccessFactors People Analytics  SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: How to print Rebate Credit Memo with settlement document type in S/4 HANA 2023
entry.content:
I have created a settlement document type for a Customer. The rebate credit memo amount is also created properly. Now, I need to print the rebate credit memo and send it to the customer. How to achieve it?

 

DRS

entry.title: Re: User/Approver did not receive email notification for...
entry.content:
Hi there! 

Is there a chance that the intended recipient was not yet in the associated role or RCM related group/pool when the info/workflow was initiated?

We've seen that happen in our sys before. The easiest fix is to have the Talent Acquisition group or Requisition creator/owner add the intended recipient to the target pool/group at the associated level. 

It may not resolve this particular req, or you may be able to reinitiate by changing/resetting the requisition status and resend. 

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Increase the useful life of an asset with a new depre...
entry.content:
Yes this is possible, you should be able to enter a new useful life into a new interval. 

Can you take some screenshots and attach

entry.title: Re: IT Depreciation is not calculating correctly in Clous...
entry.content: Refer to the SAP help, all steps are explained https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/cdba0feaf54a493385fe0394122202c6.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Tcode F-90 / App with the same funtionality in SAP Pu...
entry.content:
Hi dear college,

Please have a look on the link below, probably you will find a solution for your requirement.

Post Asset Acquisition - And Quantity Adjustment | SAP Help Portal

Regards,

Julio

entry.title: Multistage and Late Stage Application Preview Tool
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, 

I'm curious about how other organizations configure the access to the Multistage and Late Stage Application Preview Tool. 

Is anyone giving access to HR Administrators or only limiting it to Implementation Partners?

In this KBA, it said: This tool is targeted to implementation partners during the implementation phase.

2863384 - Multistage and Late Stage Application Preview Tool - Recruiting Management - SAP for Me

Multistage and Late Stage Application Preview Tool | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP RAP : Custom Entities with compositions relationship in a Fiori Elements App
entry.content:
@vijay_kamath2 

You have to set the total number of records with the actual number of records that satisfy the filter criteria .

In your case, ideally it would be more than 20.

Then the frame work takes care to fire the backend call when you scroll down.

Best wishes,

Ramjee Korada

entry.title: Re: Z-tcode creation for CJ8G
entry.content: Background jobs will write a log with all messages.

entry.title: Re: I am unable to print multi level BOM output after mat...
entry.content:
Hi @narasimha_123 

 

We have Released CDS view available at CDS Views for Bill of Material

 

Please use these for your requirement

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Loading data to Planning enabled Model through Analytical application Scripting
entry.content:
We have Planning Model which required planning data from Excel received the data using Custom Widget table data http response, processing the data via Analytical application Button click Script below:

 

Table_1.getPlanning().setEnabled(true);

Table_1.getPlanning().setUserInput(lselection, rawVal );

Table_1.getPlanning().submitData();

Clicking the button – browser debugger shows an error.

 

Any help would be appreciated!

entry.title: Export public dimensions to another tenant in sap analytics cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Analytics Cloud experts

I would like to know if there is a way to export the public dimensions with SAP Analytics cloud own export tool, to move it from one system to another.

So far we had been using a productive system and now that we are going to implement a quality system I would like to move all the public dimensions we have in the main system to the test system.

But I have not found any way to do it in the native SAC tool.

entry.title: Re: Export public dimensions to another tenant in sap ana...
entry.content: I discovered that at the same time the export is done, if it finds a public dimension it automatically exports it.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic parallel processing in SAP S/4HANA flexible w...
entry.content: Thanks for the detailed response. I have tried multiple task instances using 'All of the recipients' needs to complete the instance. But each workitem needs to have different cost center and amounts if possible. Is there any way in step callback to update each instance task container with such values as individual cost center?

entry.title: Re: CDS views for Public cloud to the stock.
entry.content:
Hi @purvalokhande 

 

Have you tried using the CDS view I_ProductValuationBasic ?

https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/PCE_I_PRODUCTVALUATIONBASIC

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: RAR support for Phased Contracts
entry.content: Thanks @xin. does RAR have the visibility of all the phases(current and future with increased prices) of the provider contract for determining the revenue schedule and equally distribute the revenue recognition across the contract term. As per my analyses, only the current/active phase will be distributed at the provider contract and future phases only will be enabled as and when the phase is active hence RAR may not have the visibility of all the phases of the provider contract to equally distribute the revenue across the contract period(multi years). Please advise. Thanks

entry.title: SAP SMTP service with Office365 online exchange
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

We are planning to move from on-prem Microsoft exchange Relay server to Microsoft 365. 
On-Prem version is about to end, and we need to move the relay server to Microsoft 365 or else all the outgoing emails will be failed to deliver to the recipients. 

Microsoft is offering 4 options to configure but what does SAP recommends? 
FYI : The SMTP AUTH is going away as of August-September of next year. 


-SMTP Authentication Client Submission
-SMTP Direct Send
-Using Exchange Online SMTP relay connector
-Using SMTP relay server as intermediary to Exchange Online

Reference doc from Microsoft:
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/sap/workloads/exchange-online-integration-sap-email-outbound

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP RAP : Custom Entities with compositions relationship in a Fiori Elements App
entry.content:
@Ramjee_korada 

I did do exactly the same thing which you suggested, but it does not seem to work.In the below code snippet, lv_top gets a value of 20, but i_header has more than 20 entries.  This causes a problem when the control moves out of the class and SAP standard code checks if page size > number of records and throws an exception.  

 

DATA(lv_top) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).
DATA(lv_skip) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).
DATA(lt_fields) = io_request->get_requested_elements( ).
DATA(lt_sort) = io_request->get_sort_elements( ).

DATA(lv_entity_name) = io_request->get_entity_id( ).
CASE lv_entity_name.
   When '<Entity Name1>'.
      <Call API to get the details...into table i_header>

      io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( i_header ) ).
      io_response->set_data( i_header ).

entry.title: Re: Modify Z* Db table not working
entry.content:
Hi Sandra, I am testing this one. The prog is showing that its updating the same data for same 'INVNO' (key field) even if i attempt to update multiple times. EVEN showing sy-subrc is showing = 0 all the time.

But the no of entries in the Z* table remains the same .

Do i need to use 'Transporting' addition to my modify stmt?

entry.title: Re: Handling multi-page data in SAP RAP Unmanaged Query
entry.content: Hi - I am facing the same problem. Would be super helpful if you can share how you were able to solve this problem.

entry.title: How to see visible entities as effective user?
entry.content:
Dear all,

I have a project where my user has highest privilages on API. I would like to call odata API to get a list of entities to simulate what would a certain user see, for example, list of job requisitions that target user would see. I understand there is `checkUserPermissions` which could help me, but i don't know how to use it in my case. Which specific parameters should I send to get list of job requisitions that user X can see?

I appreciate any help I can get.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Implementation Team Structure for Success
entry.content: Thanks for sharing, the stakeholder map should be added as an Activate Asset for HXM implementation

entry.title: Eliminar Documentos Pendientes de Autorizacion
entry.content:
Buen día a todos,

Tengo una Orden de Venta con Status Pendiente de Autorización, sin embargo, no la puedo Cerrar ni Cancelar desde la transacción ni desde el informe de Status de Autorización.

Me pueden ayudar con alguna otra alternativa o algún Query. 

Gracias.

entry.title: Re: PBO FLOW LOGIC FOR TABLECONTROL - change some fields in table editable
entry.content: I know where was mistake. The code is correct, but in next module code change screen on group. Thank you for attention.

entry.title: Re: fiori elements : List Report aggregate function
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: ODATA in C4C Analytics
entry.content:
Excellent blog about Odata, very explanatory and contains a lot of useful information. I am facing a problem when trying to extract data from a sales quote report, when filtering a lot of data the system returns an internal server error below:

DataSource.Error: Microsoft.Mashup.Engine1.Library.Resources.HttpResource: Request failed:
OData Version: 3 and 4, Error: The remote server returned an error: (500) Internal Server Error. (Internal Server Error)
OData Version: 4, Error: The remote server returned an error: (406) Not Acceptable. (Not Acceptable)
OData Version: 3, Error: The remote server returned an error: (500) Internal Server Error. (Internal Server Error)
Details:
DataSourceKind=OData

Any tips?

entry.title: Build and Deploy Your First SAP Commerce Cloud Project
entry.content:
Overview

SAP Commerce Cloud provides a lot of self-service features to configure, build and deploy a commerce solution. When you're first getting started it could seem like a lot to take in. In this article we'll take you through step-by-step how to use the self-service features to configure your application, followed by how to build and deploy to your development environment. After following the steps in this article you should be comfortable with adding in any additional customizations and deploying them to your environment of choice. Before going through this article we do recommend you review the Initial Setup and Storefront Validation section of the product documentation, which this article expands upon.

Table of Contents

  • Initial Access to SAP Cloud Portal
  • Connect an Empty Git Repository
  • Set Up Your Git Repository
    • Get it Working Locally
    • Review and Configure Your Build Manifest
    • Commit to Your Git Repository
  • Secure Your Solution
  • The Initial Build
  • The Initial Deploy
  • Monitor your Application
  • Removing the Accelerator
  • Conclusion

Initial Access to SAP Cloud Portal

Before you even think of building and deploying a project, you should first take a moment to get acquainted with the Cloud Portal, which is the self-service tool for managing your SAP Commerce Cloud solution. To access Cloud Portal, you'll need to have an SAP User ID (S-user) and have been granted access by someone with a Customer System Admin role. If you're not sure who your Super Admin might be, you can navigate to https://me.sap.com/userscontacts/usermanagement, select "Important Contacts" and then look for someone with the "Super Administrator" role. They can then grant you access like in the following video:

https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/How+to+Manage+Users+in+the+Cloud+Portal-+SAP+Commerce+Cloud/1_xipd5sh1

Assuming you're either a super admin or someone has granted you a role you can go to https://portal.commerce.ondemand.com/. If you have trouble with your password see this page in the product documentation. Make sure you're also using a supported browser.

Once you're logged in, you should make sure you have some environments provisioned. You can do so by clicking on the "Environments" button in the top left of the Cloud Portal. You should have at least 1 development, 1 stage and 1 production environment (if you require more environments, they are available for purchase). If you do not see any environments provisioned then you will need to find your Super Admin to use the self-service environment provisioning in SAP for Me (please consult the onboarding documentation for a step-by-step guide for environment provisioning)

Connect an Empty Git Repository

The next step is to connect your empty Git repository you will be using for maintaining the code which will be built and deployed on SAP Commerce Cloud. Each subscription for SAP Commerce Cloud comes with the ability to connect to a single Git-based repository. The git repository needs to be on a public accessible server(not behind a firewall in a corporate environment). Access through SSH and HTTPS methods are both possible. The following video shows how to connect to a Git repository:

https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/How+to+Connect+Your+Code+Repository+to+Cloud+Portal-+SAP+Commerce+Cloud/1_k7dw7g1m

Initial Access to SAP Cloud Portal

Before you even think of building and deploying a project, you should first take a moment to get acquainted with the Cloud Portal, which is the self-service tool for managing your SAP Commerce Cloud solution. To access Cloud Portal, you'll need to have an SAP User ID (S-user) and have been granted access by someone with a Customer System Admin role. If you're not sure who your Super Admin might be, you can navigate to https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/user/management, select "Important Contacts" and then look for someone with the "Super Administrator" role. They can then grant you access like in the following video:

https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/How+to+Manage+Users+in+the+Cloud+Portal-+SAP+Commerce+Cloud/1_xipd5sh1

Assuming you're either a super admin or someone has granted you a role you can go to https://portal.commerce.ondemand.com/. If you have trouble with your password see this page in the product documentation. Make sure you're also using a supported browser.

Once you're logged in, you should make sure you have some environments provisioned. You can do so by clicking on the "Environments" button in the top left of the Cloud Portal. You should have at least 1 development, 1 stage and 1 production environment (if you require more environments, they are available for purchase). If you do not see any environments provisioned then you will need to find your Super Admin to use the self-service environment provisioning in SAP for Me (please consult the onboarding documentation for a step-by-step guide for environment provisioning)

Connect an Empty Git Repository

The next step is to connect your empty Git repository you will be using for maintaining the code which will be built and deployed on SAP Commerce Cloud. Each subscription for SAP Commerce Cloud comes with the ability to connect to a single Git-based repository. The git repository needs to be on a public accessible server(not behind a firewall in a corporate environment). Access through SSH and HTTPS methods are both possible. The following video shows how to connect to a Git repository:

https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/How+to+Connect+Your+Code+Repository+to+Cloud+Portal-+SAP+Commerce+Cloud/1_k7dw7g1m

Set Up Your Git Repository

Now that your Git repository is connected, this section will help take your through the steps to populate the repository with code. There is an extensive section in the product documentation with various ways you can populate the repository, but if you're looking for a working template to get you started there are various code samples you can download to initially populate your repository.  this section will cover how to get started with sample code which contains the structure for a working Spartacus-based storefront as well as a sample accelerator-based storefront.

Get it Working Locally

Given your development team will most likely be developing and testing customizations locally, you should try to structure your repository to allow for building both locally and on your SAP Commerce Cloud environments. To do this, you'll need to keep in mind a few things:

  • Ensure you're using your .gitignore file to only upload items to your repository that aren't automatically added as part of the SAP Commerce Cloud build automation process. For example, you shouldn't be adding the SAP Commerce "bin" folder as the relevant extensions you declare in your manifest file (see below) will be automatically added as part of the build.
  • The sample code assumes you will want to develop with Cloud Hot Folders locally. If you're on SAP Commerce core version 2005 the sap-ccv2-hotfolder module is included. If you're on Commerce core 1905 you will need to get the extensions from Cloud Extension Pack. Even if you're not using the optional Cloud Extension Packs, you need to download the artifact, as you will need the sap-ccv2-hotfolder module that comes with it. If you're using Cloud Extension Packs then you will need all the code. To download search for "CX Comm" in launchpad.support.sap.com and select the "Ext Pack" for your version. See Testing Cloud Hot Folders in a Local Environment in the product documentation for more. If you're on Commerce core 6.7-1811 then see this SAP Note.
  • You'll need a copy of the latest patched version of SAP Commerce core that you'll be using. See this page to find out how to download.

Now that you're ready to get started locally, do the following:

      1. Clone your empty git repository where you will be storing your code
      2. (optional) If you want to start off your code review process correctly from the very beginning, you may want to create both a 'develop' and  'feature' branch where you'll be storing your code
      3. Use our open source sample repo to pick a branch and download a zip of this code sample. Note: the code sample repository contains multiple branches, each covering a distinct use case (data hub, spartacus, vanilla). You may choose to select a different branch, but for this example we are using the Spartacus branch
      4. Unzip the sample code file into where you cloned your git repository
      5. In a different location unzip a copy of your SAP Commerce core
      6. Move the following folders from the unzipped SAP Commerce core directory into the core-customize/hybris/bin from step #4
        1. hybris/bin/modules
        2. hybris/bin/platform
      7. (Optional) If you're using Cloud Extension Pack then unzip and copy the contents of "hybris/bin/modules/" into the "hybris/bin/modules-cep" directory from step #4
      8. Complete the steps for the following sections of the installation steps in the product documentation:

        1. Setting up Apache Ant

      9. Run the following code that is dependent on your version to ensure your add-ons are loaded correctly:

        1. For SAP Commerce 2011:
          ant addoninstall -Daddonnames="promotionenginesamplesaddon,smarteditaddon,textfieldconfiguratortemplateaddon,assistedservicestorefront,assistedservicepromotionaddon,customerticketingaddon,orderselfserviceaddon,adaptivesearchsamplesaddon,multicountrysampledataaddon,pcmbackofficesamplesaddon,personalizationaddon" -DaddonStorefront.yacceleratorstorefront="yacceleratorstorefront"
        2. For SAP Commerce 2005:
          ant addoninstall -Daddonnames="spartacussampledataaddon,commerceorgsamplesaddon,promotionenginesamplesaddon,smarteditaddon,textfieldconfiguratortemplateaddon,assistedservicestorefront,assistedservicepromotionaddon,customerticketingaddon,orderselfserviceaddon,adaptivesearchsamplesaddon,multicountrysampledataaddon,pcmbackofficesamplesaddon,hybrisanalyticsaddon,personalizationsearchsamplesaddon,personalizationaddon" -DaddonStorefront.yacceleratorstorefront="yacceleratorstorefront"
        3. For SAP Commerce core version <=1905 run the following:
          ant addoninstall -Daddonnames="acceleratorwebservicesaddon,cmsoccaddon" -DaddonStorefront.ycommercewebservices="ycommercewebservices"
      10. Continue with the following sections of the installation steps in the product documentation:

        1. Performing the Build Procedure

        2. Starting the Application Server and Deploying the Application

        3. Initializing SAP Commerce

      11. (Optional) If you want to have a local Blob storage instance for testing out Cloud Hot Folders you can go into hybris/config/local.properties file and un-comment the lines with the following keys:

        cluster.node.groups
        azure.hotfolder.storage.container.hotfolder
        azure.hotfolder.storage.account.connection-string

If you accidentally uncomment these without setting up a local Blob storage your solution will still start, but you'll just see a lot of errors in your logs like "Connection refused (Connection refused): 127.0.0.1:10000"

12. You should now have your SAP Commerce solution running locally

13. Once your SAP Commerce Cloud solution is running locally (e.g. you can get to localhost:9001/hac) it's now time to get your Spartacus storefront built and running locally. To do this navigate to the js-storefront/spartacusstore

14. Execute the following command, which will build and run your storefront:

yarn build
yarn start

15. You should now have local instances running of SAP Commerce and the Spartacus storefront. To confirm the Spartacus storefront is working please navigate to http://localhost:4200

Review and Configure Your Build Manifest

The Build Manifests enable you to configure your SAP Commerce Cloud solution to your exact specifications. You can define the build process using a manifest.json file, which is stored in your code repository. A sample manifest.json file has been included as part of the sample code we provided above. For this step you should review the manifest file to ensure you understand what each of the components are for and how the sample is configured. It is recommended that you minimize the changes in the beginning to ensure it works first with the Cloud Automation tooling. Some changes you likely would make in the future include

    • commerceSuiteVersion - which version of SAP Commerce Core you're using
    • useCloudExtensionPack - if you want to enable Cloud Extension Packs
    • storefrontAddons - if you have any additional add-on extensions you've enabled in your localextensions.xml file you should ensure they are included in your manifest
    • aspects -  define any properties and webapps for any of the pre-defined aspects
    • tests - if you have any tests within your extensions you would like to run as part of the Cloud Automation builds you should include them here

Commit to Your Git Repository

At this point you can now build locally, so you should commit and push your code to your git repository.

Secure Your Solution

Before you build and deploy your solution you should take a moment to familiarize yourself with the various security configurations in Cloud Portal and ideally actually start configuring them to meet your business needs.

 

 

 

For more, watch the following video:

https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/1_ugnfs2pj

IP Filter Sets

By default, for each of your environments the endpoints are set to "Deny All" access. You can leverage IP Filter sets to determine which IPs should have access to each of your endpoints. The IPs will likely be different for each endpoint. Some recommendations:

        • Storefront endpoint will normally be the most open of all your endpoints (you want people to come to your store), so you could create an IP filter set that limits any IPs you do not want to have access to your store (e.g. you may have requirements to restrict access to certain countries) and then you would set your endpoint to "Allow All"
        • API endpoint should use the same IP filter as your storefront
        • Backoffice endpoint is normally an internal-only endpoint. In this case you would choose to remain with "Deny All" but your IP Filter set would include all IPs of your business (including VPNs)
        • BackgroundProcessing endpoint isn't something you'll be accessing often, but should be treated similar to the backoffice endpoint above. You may want to access the endpoint for admin/troubleshooting, so ensure you either use the same IP filter set as backoffice or have one that is even more restrictive
        • Solr endpoint is also something typically restricted to admin/troubleshooting, so you should leave the default "Deny All" and use either the same IP Filter as your backoffice and/or backgroundProcessing endpoints. 

SSL Certificates

Although SAP Commerce Cloud provides default SSL certificates for each of its endpoints, it is recommended that you look into using your own. If you already have SSL certificates you should take a moment to add them now and update your endpoint to point to the new certificate. This way you're testing right from the start, with your certificate.

Trusted Certificates / Host Alias

If you're going to be working with external services that require special certificates you can take this opportunity to load the certificates and set up the host alias sets. Given our example doesn't have any third party integrations this configuration is more optional for the moment.

Security Files

You can use the "Security Files" page in Cloud Portal to safely upload certificates, passwords, salts, tokens, and other security files to your subscription. You can then attach them to your environment so they will be picked up as part of the deployment. 

Sensitive Properties

Although the sample code we provided contains environment specific "local.properties" files, there is often the case where you have certain properties that are very sensitive and you don't want to check in to your source control (e.g. passwords). You won't need this for this initial deployment, but it is good to get acquainted with the process for future deployments. One option is to use static files for your sensitive data. The other option is to define the properties as part of each environment's service configuration. The downside for this option is that anyone with customer sys admin role in Cloud Portal will be able to see these properties.

The Initial Build

At this point you've confirmed your code is working locally, it's checked into your Git repository, you've configured your endpoints and managed your security settings. The next step is to build the code. The steps are outlined on this page of the product documentation. If for some reason your build fails you can see the build log, fix any issues and retry the build.

 

 

 

The Initial Deploy

Once your build is successful you should first try deploying it to your development environment. Given it's your first deployment you should set it to "initialize database".

 

 

 

Once the deployment is complete you can open up the JS Storefront endpoint's URL and you should see the Project Spartacus storefront.

If you missed any of the build/deploy steps above, you can also watch the following video, which provides a walkthrough of the process:

https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/How+to+Create+and+Deploy+a+Build+in+the+Cloud+Portal+-+SAP+Commerce+Cloud/1_j1ffp373

Monitor your Application

Dynatrace

Each environment comes with an instance of Dynatrace Application Performance Monitoring. You should take a few moments to familiarize yourself with Dynatrace, as it is a very useful tool for monitoring your application as well as troubleshooting when issues arise. In the video below we show how to access Dynatrace:

https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/1_5i0xt6zz

Centralized Logging

Each SAP Commerce Cloud environment comes with its own logging endpoint. You should take this opportunity to setup your logging solution as it can also be very helpful for troubleshooting future issues.

Removing the Accelerator

In the sample code we've also included the configurations for an accelerator-based storefront, which you can access by clicking on the Storefront endpoint's URL. If you do not want to have an accelerator storefront:

        1. In the "aspects" section of the manifest.json file remove the "accstorefront" section
        2. On each environment's page in Cloud Portal delete the "Storefront" endpoint by clicking on the trash can. In the future if you want to bring it back, you can always add a new endpoint.

See the following video which covers more about managing endpoints:

https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/How+to+Manage+Endpoints+in+the+Cloud+Portal-+SAP+Commerce+Cloud/1_2dht3gok

Conclusion

You should now have a running application in your development environment. At this point the possibilities are endless, If you're looking for more expert recommendations feel free to look at all the CRM and CX Blogs by SAP articles we have for SAP Commerce Cloud, as well as the best practices outlined in the product documentation.

Disclaimer: Original version of this article was published in SAP CX Works.

entry.title: Re: Purchasing unit not being respected when converting P...
entry.content: Thanks for responding - in testing it appears that both ME21N and ME21 both have the same results (they are both working properly).

entry.title: Re: Weigh Bridge integration in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone share the gate in gate out developed screenshot.

Regards 

Nafees

entry.title: Re: SAP ABAP RAP : Custom Entities with compositions relationship in a Fiori Elements App
entry.content: I guess I replied too soon :).  I have figured out the issue.  Basically for set_data, I need to set only the required number of entries supported per page (as per page size), but for set_total_number_of_records, it should be the total number of records.

entry.title: Re: Purchasing unit not being respected when converting P...
entry.content: This has been solved - the "Variable Purchase Order Unit" was set to active in the purchase info record (not the material master). When turning this indicator from "1" (Active) to blank (Not Active), PPDS works correctly and the purchasing unit is respected during Purchase Order creation.

entry.title: Re: Third party delivery process for subcontracting raw m...
entry.content:
Dear all,

We would like to reiterate that the information previously provided in this "Third party subcontracting" question remains valid and applicable.

After review, we confirm that there have been no changes or updates that impact the validity of the data previously provided.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Hi, can anyone please let me know an Alternate feature of Spell Check
entry.content: Hi @kajal_sahu1 My company uses Google Chrome as our default browser, and there is a spell check setting we have on as a default right within Chrome. There is a similar setting in Firefox. I have also used the free version of the program called Grammarly for both spelling and grammar checking.

entry.title: BW aDSO - activate table changes
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

We've got a list of aDSO's which are used for customizing tables in planning applications or as mapping tables. The request from the business/external auditors is now to provide log of table changes for that tables, so that evaluation can be done by table DBTABLOG and its standard transaction SCU3.

We thought we could activate option "Log Changes" by transaction SE13 for /BIC/A...2 tables in our development system. When we did that test, we faced the issue that change is not added to transport. So we activated aDSO by Eclipse and by that transport entry was created. But after that option "Log Changes" is deactivated again.

In Prod system we are not able to change that option, because of mandant status.

How to activate "Log Changes" option for aDSO's to provide external auditors table log?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BW aDSO - activate table changes
entry.content:
These information information can be found as per links below:

Log Changes to Personal Data = BPC only

Audit

entry.title: Re: Internal error in LMLCCS_UPDATEF02 LCL_DB method get_...
entry.content:
Hello,

I am also sharing another document that may help you with this activity:

"MIGO Error : Message no. MLCCS009"

Kind regards,

entry.title: Buddy App
entry.content:
SAP should comeup with a buddy section or a Buddy app
With consultants spread across different geographies, its tough to find people to network to discuss whats new and not in SAP world

The buddy app should give options what all topic of interest, i have planned 4 certifications this year but could not progress on any
Just a thought, Buddy apps can be very helpful in the Corporate world too, people can volunteer to become Buddies to new joiners within companies too. The app should connect to the corporate guest wifi and should be able to connect with the active directory
Not everyone can groom a new joiner to fit the Organization

Just a thought!

entry.title: How to pass header and line item xml body to the CPI Endpoint?
entry.content:
Hello,

Please suggest, how to manage the header and multiple line item data dynamically in SAP CPI.

entry.title: How to Find and Adjust the Text Types for a Transaction Type?
entry.content:
IT Service Management, Change control management, or Focused Build are in use.

Information is needed on how to find which text determination procedure is in use for a particular transaction type, and how it can be adjusted to add or remove text types?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Find and Adjust the Text Types for a Transacti...
entry.content:
To find the current text determination procedure in use:

  1. Execute transaction code CRMC_PROCESS_MA
  2. Find the transaction type in question (ie. ZMIN)
  3. Double click, then scroll to "Profiles"
  4. Take note of the entry in "Text Det. Procedure" (ie. SMIN0001)

To adjust the text determination procedure:

  1. Navigate to SPRO -> SAP Solution Manager -> Capabilities -> IT Service Management -> Text Determination Procedure -> Define Text Determination Procedure
  2. Select CRM_ORDERH
  3. Click on "Procedure"
  4. Find the procedure from the first section (ie. SMIN0001)
  5. Click on "Definition of procedure"
  6. From here you can adjust the text types available in the determination procedure

Note: In some cases the text determination procedure is used by multiple transaction types. Like in the example above SMIN0001 is used by both SMIN and ZMIN. If texts need to be adjusted for ZMIN, a new text determination procedure should be defined and assigned to ZMIN in order to avoid customization being overwritten with upgrades and updates to the standard in the system.

entry.title: Error while extending Fiori App "Manage Maintenance Backlog". Error : Navigation Not allowed
entry.content: How to extend Manage Maintenance Backlog to add few indicators

entry.title: Re: How to pass header and line item xml body to the CPI Endpoint?
entry.content: Manage header and multiple line items dynamically with respect to what or part of what process?

entry.title: Re: How to pass header and line item xml body to the CPI Endpoint?
entry.content: order no and its line item

entry.title: SAC Live connection with SAP BW without authentication
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to create a direct live connection between SAC and SAP BW without any authentication.

We are not using SSO and also our BW is on premise. However when choosing the option, "None" under 'Authentication Method' after creating the connection, it does not allow to open any dashboard created with this connection. Can you please clarify if this functionality is obsolete or can we achieve this somehow without SSO?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAC Live connection with SAP BW without authentication
entry.content:
When creating a connection in SAC to a backend system, the user have to be authenticated on the backend system. This can be done in two ways: user input credentials manually or it is authenticated by SSO.
Therefore the option, "None" cannot be achieved without SSO.
Refer to: Using the 'None' Authentication Option

entry.title: LOGON 770
entry.content: hello i could not download hana gui logon 770 for windows. i would be grateful to you if you send me the link. thank you very much in advance

entry.title: Re: Syntax Error in GRC Dev standard code
entry.content:
Hi,

When entering the NWBC transaction of the DEV GRC system and wanting to assign a firefighter, I get the error.

entry.title: Re: Batch Odata GET operation to get SAP C4C Sales Quote Data from different collection
entry.content:
Excellent day, Could you help me, I need to make the same selection but only bringing some specific fields, for example for the filter: sap/c4c/odata/v1/c4codataapi/SalesQuoteCollection?$filter=eq ID '376231'&$expand=SalesQuoteItem

I need to filter certain fields in the SalesQuote collection with SalesQuoteItem, is it possible? When trying the filter below I was unsuccessful!

/sap/c4c/odata/v1/c4codataapi/SalesQuoteCollection?$Select=ID,Name$filter=ID eq '376231'&$expand=SalesQuoteItem&format=json

How to add $select for second base SalesQuoteItem?

entry.title: Supplier Evaluation Metrics for Scorecard
entry.content:
Good Afternoon all, I am in the process of developing a vendor scorecard using data from SAP. I have the following tiles: 

  • Supplier Evaluation by Quantity
  • Supplier Evaluation by Time
  • Operational supplier Evaluation 
  • Supplier Evaluation by price
  • Overall Supplier Evaluation 
  • Supplier evaluation by questionnaire
  • Overall supplier evaluation 

I have a couple of questions. First off the supplier evaluation by Delivery Time states it uses the Good Receipt date and the delivery date, However we update the delivery date on a single line so the other departments know when the material is supposed to arrive. In the evaluation of the score, does it use the original delivery date and good received date or does it use the updated delivery date?

My next question is regarding the quantity metric. First, to my knowledge this metric is using the amount ordered and amount received. However if the supplier cannot fill the initial quantity on the PO, and they split it into 2 orders (ex: ordered 100, and received separate shipments of 75 and 25). Is the score going to penalize the vendor for this as we did not receive the initial ordered amount of 100?

The evaluation of quality is based upon the lot number according my program. Is this a good measure of quality? I thought about possibly measuring the quality by the returns/rejected materials but I am not sure how to integrate this into SAP

My next question is how to implement a questionnaire in SAP and use it as a metric in a scorecard.

My last question is other tips or guidance on creating a scorecard and if its possible relying solely on SAP for this. Thank you in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Re: SAP Print Service
entry.content: Can SAP Print Service be used with S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition? Does SAP support this scenario?

entry.title: Re: Report to display list of WBS & AUC
entry.content: Thanks very much

entry.title: Request for digital skills initiative by SAP Learning
entry.content:
Hi David

First of all thanks for this digital skills initiative by SAP Learning.
I am interested in taking this learning opportunity.

Name : Mohana Priya
Location : India
Experience : Have prior SAP experience - 15 + yrs
Technology :SAP PI ,ABAP
Currently : Not employed for  2 + years due to career break ( personal / family situation & kids)

Interested in learning - CPI , ABAP BTP and HANA basics  

I fall under category Women .(Unemployed currently due to Career - break )

As I have a career break for past 2 + years , now I am lagging in technology updates  .
Due to family situations and needs soon I am planning to find a new career opportunity  ,
so I am interested in upskilling the latest technology updates and also to explore
more in SAP relevant for my past work experience .

Requesting you to consider this and so the needful , thanks in advance .

Thanks & Regards
Mohana

entry.title: How to add new tab at item level to MIGO
entry.content: The following link seemed promising: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/how-to-create-a-custom-tab-for-migo-item-details/ba-p/13260106.  However, I am not able to complete steps three and four when it talks about appending my structures.  The system will not let me append the structures because they already exist.  Am I doing something wrong, or do I just implement the BADI and try to figure out how to create a new tab with new fields for entering and displaying the entered data?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: Thank you, @Maheshwar_Singh - we are working with SAP Support on the issue we are experiencing. Also thank you for the link update. 🙂

entry.title: ERROR IN PRINTOUT TO MATERIAL DOCUMENT OVERVIEW APP SAP HANA CLOUD PUBLIC EDITION
entry.content:
I hope everybody is fine

I hope you can help me with the following problem.

I used to app “ MATERIAL DOCUMENT OVERVIEW” an choose the detail item an verify the “material document item” and I select the  “output management” tab the print  doesn’t appear with available.

 

 

 

 

Also, check the app “ OUTPUT PARAMETER DETERMINATION” with  rules for “Good Movement and   Determination step and when I choose “MMIM_MATDOC_GDSMVMT_ESOA”  with channel EDI  appear the next message

 

 

 

I treat to change the channel to PRINTOUT , but is impossible to modify. (error message, chanel  EDI for “MMIM_MATDOC_GDSMVMT_ESOA”   is mandatory)

 

Could you help me to know the possible solution to printing the materials document?  this situation exists in SAP HANA CLOUD PUBLIC EDITION

SAP HANA Cloud  #SAPHANAPUBLICEDITION 

Thank you so much.
